== Notes for [[Item:Q116566|Q116566]] ==

Bigorra was a feudal jurisdiction of Aquitaine in the region of Tarba (Tarbes), which took its name from a castle. Bigorra (Bigorre in French) is a region of Gascony, which today occupies part of the department of Hautes-Pyrénées (France) extending from the Pyrenees to the plain of Tarba.
--------------------
García Arnaldo, comte de Bigorre1

b. circa 972

Father Arnaldo I, comte de Bigorre b. circa 950

Charts Descendants of Charlemage

García Arnaldo, comte de Bigorre was born circa 972. He was the son of Arnaldo I, comte de Bigorre. García Arnaldo, comte de Bigorre married Ricar d' Astarac, daughter of Guillermo I d' Astarac.2

Family

Ricar d' Astarac b. circa 975

Child

Gersinde, comtesse de Bigorre+ b. c 995, d. a 10382

Citations

[S204] Roderick W. Stuart, RfC, 227-34.

[S1265] Genealogy.eu, online genealogy.euweb.cz, Counts of Bigorre, Ribagorza and Pailhars.

Name Prefix:<NPFX> Count Name Suffix:<NSFX> of Bigorre

[Brøderbund WFT Vol. 2, Ed. 1, Tree #1241, Date of Import: May 8, 1997]

!COUNT OF BIGORRE

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
